content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.5 `Claim` .. 3.6 `Profile`
Document lines: 311-417

### 3.5 `Claim`

Атомарный факт или relation edge.

Это ключевая таблица для:
- точных factual answers
- predicate-based reconciliation
- explainable retrieval

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `claim_id: uuid`
- `namespace_id`
- `subject_entity_id: uuid`
- `predicate: text`
- `object_entity_id: uuid | null`
- `object_value_json: jsonb | null`
- `normalized_text: text`
- `context_text: text | null`
- `scope_json: jsonb`
- `qualifiers_json: jsonb`
- `confidence: numeric(4,3)`
- `importance: smallint`
- `status: active | superseded | retracted | expired | candidate`
- `retrieval_state: active | warm | cold | archived | deleted`
- `valid_from: timestamptz | null`
- `valid_to: timestamptz | null`
- `origin_note_id: uuid | null`
- `first_seen_at: timestamptz`
- `last_seen_at: timestamptz`
- `supersedes_claim_id: uuid | null`
- `retracted_by_claim_id: uuid | null`
- `created_from_run_id: uuid | null`
- `created_at: timestamptz`
- `updated_at: timestamptz`
- `embedding: vector | null`
- `use_count: integer`
- `last_used_at: timestamptz | null`
- `last_validated_at: timestamptz | null`
- `archived_at: timestamptz | null`

Отдельная таблица `claim_sources`:
- `claim_id`
- `source_id`
- `support_type: direct | inferred | imported | derived_from_note`
- `evidence_span`
- `evidence_quote`
- `support_confidence`

Инварианты:
- один claim = одно predicate application
- claim должен иметь смысл без исходного чата
- explicit statement > inference
- relative time должен быть нормализован
- если смысл еще слишком мягкий, лучше `Note`, чем плохой `Claim`

---

### 3.6 `Profile`

Компактная always-in-context проекция.

Это не source of truth, а projection layer поверх:
- explicit user instructions
- stable claims
- selected long-lived context

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `profile_id: uuid`
- `namespace_id`
- `owner_entity_id: uuid`
- `profile_json: jsonb`
- `profile_text: text`
- `version: bigint`
- `updated_from_run_id: uuid | null`
- `last_compacted_at: timestamptz | null`
- `created_at`
- `updated_at`

Рекомендуемая структура:

```json
{
  "preferred_name": null,
  "languages": [],
  "timezone": null,
  "response_preferences": {
    "response_language": null,
    "tone": null,
    "detail_level": null,
    "code_comment_language": null
  },
  "expertise": [],
  "stable_preferences": [],
  "constraints": [],
  "current_goals": [],
  "soft_signals": []
}
```

Инварианты:
- profile должен быть коротким
- explicit facts важнее inference
- спорные facts лучше оставлять в claim/note, а не тащить в profile
- profile должен быть пересобираем

---

Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля' keywords=scope_text,scope_json,retrieval_state,truth status,usage fields tags=cross_cutting,retrieval_lifecycle,schema_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Source` component role and invariants in v3 data model summary=In section 3.1, `Source` is defined as the raw knowledge/evidence layer that stores chat/tool/imported/external records and supports re-check/explainability; it should be immutable in practice, keep `observed_at` distinct from ingestion time, use `content_hash` for dedupe, and enforce explicit deletion policy. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Source,evidence layer,observed_at,content_hash,delete policy tags=data_model,memory_v3,source_component useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 memory model layers and design goals summary=The v3 data model is designed to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability by splitting memory into layers: Source (evidence), Entity/Claim (semantic graph), Note (intermediate), Profile/Task/Episode (operational projections), and MemoryRun (audit). scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=v3 data model,memory layers,typed memory,lineage,temporal truth tags=data_model,architecture,memory_v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Note` entity role, boundaries, and invariants in v3 summary=`Note` is an intermediate semantic memory fragment used for important retrieval-useful context that should not yet be hardened into final claims; it excludes source records and chain-of-thought, and follows invariants like one semantic fragment per note and self-contained meaning. scope=Applies to section 3.4 `Note` in 02. Data Model (agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md) keywords=Note,intermediate memory,retrieval,invariants,candidate_claims_json tags=data_model,note_semantics,v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Entity` as canonical memory node with alias/merge semantics summary=In section 3.2, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies mentions of the same object and anchors claims/tasks/notes/episodes; it uses aliases and merge status while preserving history, keeping aliases pointing to the current canonical entity, and allowing erroneous merges to be reversed via audit trail. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Entity,canonical node,entity_aliases,merge,audit trail tags=data_model,memory_v3,entity_component useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`PredicateCatalog` is a reconciliation config, not a memory item summary=In the v3 data model, `PredicateCatalog` is defined as configuration for reconciliation logic rather than a stored memory item; it encodes per-predicate rules such as cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict handling, and projection sync flags. scope=Applies to section 3.3 `PredicateCatalog` in 02. Data Model (agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md) keywords=PredicateCatalog,reconciliation,cardinality,temporal_policy,conflict_policy tags=data_model,reconciliation_config,v3 useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
